rulings.Case Title: DICK FRIEND VS. BAKER HUGHES OILFIELD OPERATIONS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross Complaint Against Third Party
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 25, 2008, line 10. DEFENDANTS ENSCO OFFSHORE COMPANY AND ENSCO HOLDING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross Complaint Against Third Party. Denied. Defendant had the ability to discover these claims at the outset of the litigation. To allow the cross-complaint would prejudice plaintiff and cross-complainants. Defendant may pursue this claim in a separate action. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: DEBORA MARTIN VS. SERGIO IANTORNO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Special Motion To Strike Various Causes Of Action In Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 25, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANTS SERGIO IANTORNO and GOLDEN PROPERTIES, L L C'S Special Motion To Strike Various Causes Of Action In Second Amended Complaint. The motion is granted as to causes of action 7 and 12. Plaintiff demonstrated that protected conduct is alleged, i.e. the prosecution of an unlawful detainer action, and plaintiff did not present evidence that plaintiff could prevail on either claim. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: DEBORA MARTIN VS. SERGIO IANTORNO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 25, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT SERGIO IANTORNO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Causes of action are barred by collateral estoppel based on plaintiff's prior quiet title actions. Murphy v. Murphy (July 22, 2008) 1st Appelate District A115177. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 13th through 16th causes of action for plaintiff did not oppose the demurrer. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: RESTAURANTS INTERNATIONAL, INC. A CORPORATION et al VS. NANCY MAR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 25, 2008, line 9. DEFENDANT NANCY MAR'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Matter is taken under submission. No appearances necessary. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. ALLEGHENY CASUALTY COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 25, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT ALLEGHENY CASUALTY COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Denied. The Court did not lack jurisdiction to forfeit bail on October 30, 2007; that was when Mr. Hood was required to appear and failed to do so. =(302/PJM/PB)
